metadata.title: On-chain address tracking - threat actors, state actors, sanctioned entities, and government-seized wallets (OFAC SDN / FBI PSAs / Treasury / chain-forensics)
metadata.frame: Crypto's transparency cuts both ways: threat actors, governments, and sanctioned entities all hold ON-CHAIN addresses whose flows are publicly traceable. This block is the attribution layer - the documented address clusters and the authoritative published lists - so the persons/firm graph connects to verifiable on-chain identities. The point: state-grade theft (DPRK), sanctions evasion (Russia/Iran), mixing (Tornado Cash), and state SEIZURE (US government Bitcoin) are all legible on-chain, and the project should track them as a first-class data source rather than rely on press summaries.
metadata.WARNING: Attribution grade matters. Government attributions (FBI PSA, OFAC SDN designation, Treasury, DOJ indictment) are HIGH-grade primary. Chain-forensics CLUSTERING (Chainalysis/Elliptic/TRM/Arkham) is probabilistic heuristic inference - strong but labeled. This block does NOT hand-transcribe long hex addresses (transcription-error risk); it cites the AUTHORITATIVE machine-readable lists (OFAC SDN crypto-address set; FBI PSA IOC lists) and records cluster-level facts (amount, date, attributor, method). 'Sanctioned' status is time-varying (e.g., Tornado Cash sanctioned 2022, vacated/lifted 2025) and is dated. Overlay edges (surveillance_security / regulatory_legal) excluded from the SCC/Z3/TLA+ proofs.
metadata.method: OFAC SDN List (sanctions.gov; the SDN entries carry 'Digital Currency Address' fields - the canonical machine-readable source). FBI/CISA PSAs and joint cyber advisories (IOC address lists). Treasury press releases (designations). DOJ indictments/forfeitures (seizure wallets). Chain-forensics blogs (Chainalysis/Elliptic/TRM/Arkham) for clustering. Each cell graded by attribution source.
metadata.ingest_target: OFAC SDN crypto-address set is downloadable and machine-readable; a fetch_ofac.py (env-free) could pull the 'Digital Currency Address - XBT/ETH/...' fields into data/ofac_crypto_addresses.json for live cross-referencing against graph entities. Wired as a roadmap item, parallel to fetch_fec/fetch_tape.

dprk_lazarus.actor: North Korea - Lazarus Group (a.k.a. TraderTraitor, APT38)
dprk_lazarus.summary: The most prolific state-grade crypto thief; proceeds fund the DPRK weapons program (per UN Panel of Experts and US Treasury). Operates through spearphish/supply-chain compromise, then launders via mixers and cross-chain bridges.

events.method: compromised a Safe{Wallet} developer machine; injected malicious JS into the multisig UI to alter transaction targets - the largest crypto heist ever.
events.attribution: FBI PSA confirmed Lazarus/TraderTraitor; FBI PUBLISHED 51 Ethereum laundering addresses and urged providers to block them.
OFAC added the Ronin-hack ETH address to the SDN list (Apr 2022) - the first time a specific hacker address was OFAC-sanctioned.
FBI attributed to Lazarus; laundered via Tornado Cash and Railgun.
FBI/chain-forensics attributed multiple 2023-24 thefts to DPRK.

dprk_lazarus.address_source: FBI PSA IOC lists (per-incident) + OFAC SDN 'Digital Currency Address' entries; cluster tracking by Chainalysis/Elliptic/Arkham.
dprk_lazarus.grade: fact (government attribution); specific laundering hops are clustering inference

russia.summary: Russian sanctions-evasion and cybercrime on-chain: sanctioned exchanges, darknet markets, and ransomware.

entities.name: Garantex
entities.detail: Russia-based exchange OFAC-sanctioned (Apr 2022) for processing illicit/ransomware funds; its on-chain addresses are on the SDN list; international action (2025) seized infrastructure and froze ~$28M+ USDT.

entities.name: Hydra Market
entities.detail: The largest darknet market, OFAC-sanctioned and seized (Apr 2022, US-German action); SDN entry includes ~100+ crypto addresses.

entities.name: Ransomware (Conti, LockBit, etc.)
entities.detail: Russia-tolerated ransomware crews receive crypto to documented wallets; OFAC has sanctioned specific ransomware-linked addresses (e.g., Chatex, SUEX, Bitzlato).

iran.summary: Iranian state-linked exchange and sanctions-evasion on-chain - and a politically-motivated counter-attack.

entities.name: Nobitex
entities.detail: Iran's largest exchange; on 2025-06-18 the pro-Israel group Predatory Sparrow (Gonjeshke Darande) drained ~$90M and BURNED it to vanity/burner addresses with no private keys (a political act, not theft-for-profit). Treasury subsequently designated Nobitex for terror-finance/sanctions-evasion; chain-forensics (TRM/Chainalysis/Elliptic) documented Nobitex's links to IRGC-adjacent flows.

mixers_and_sanctions_dynamics.summary: Mixers are the laundering chokepoint - and the legal status of sanctioning code has whipsawed.

items.name: Tornado Cash
items.detail: OFAC sanctioned it Aug 2022 (~$7B laundered since 2019, incl. DPRK funds), adding the smart-contract addresses to the SDN list - the first sanctioning of immutable code. The 5th Circuit VACATED the sanctions (Nov 2024, holding immutable smart contracts aren't sanctionable 'property'); Treasury LIFTED them Mar 21 2025. Co-developer Roman Storm prosecuted. The whole saga is the central test of whether code is sanctionable.

items.name: Blender.io / Sinbad.io
items.detail: Bitcoin mixers OFAC-sanctioned (2022/2023) for DPRK laundering; Sinbad assessed as a Blender successor.

items.name: Samourai Wallet
items.detail: Privacy wallet whose operators were arrested/charged (2024); see spec-tornado-samourai - the criminalization of privacy tooling.

government_seized_wallets.summary: The US government is itself a major on-chain holder via seizures - and is moving toward a strategic reserve.

items.detail: ~120,000 BTC stolen in 2016; DOJ seized ~$3.6B (Feb 2022) and arrested Ilya Lichtenstein and Heather 'Razzlekhan' Morgan (both pleaded guilty 2023). The seized coins sit in government-controlled wallets, periodically moved (tracked on-chain).
~$3.36B BTC seized from James Zhong (Nov 2022, the 'individual X' 2012 theft); plus the ~69,370 BTC from the 2020 forfeiture. Courts cleared the government to sell the Silk Road BTC (2025).

By 2024-25 the US government controlled on the order of ~200k BTC (~1% of supply) across seizures; a 2025 executive order established a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve (to retain seized BTC rather than sell). The state as a large, on-chain-visible BTC holder is itself a market and policy variable.
items.grade: fact (DOJ holdings) + fact (EO); exact live balance varies

edges.raw_instrument: $1.5B/401k ETH heist via Safe multisig UI compromise (FBI: 51 laundering addresses)
edges.note: OVERLAY: FBI-attributed DPRK theft, on-chain traced

edges.raw_instrument: $625M bridge hack; OFAC-sanctioned the hacker ETH address
DPRK proceeds laundered through the mixer (basis for the 2022 sanction)
sanctioned Aug 2022 (code on SDN); 5th Cir vacated 2024; lifted Mar 2025
edges.note: OVERLAY: can code be sanctioned? - whipsaw

edges.raw_instrument: SDN designation (2022) + 2025 infrastructure seizure / USDT freeze
~$90M drained and BURNED to vanity addresses (political), 2025-06-18; Treasury then designated Nobitex
edges.note: OVERLAY: state-on-state crypto strike (Israel-linked vs Iran)

edges.raw_instrument: ~$3.6B BTC seized from 2016 hack; Lichtenstein/Morgan convicted; held in gov wallets
edges.note: OVERLAY: state as on-chain holder via seizure

edges.raw_instrument: ~200k BTC (~1% supply) from seizures; 2025 Strategic Bitcoin Reserve EO (retain, not sell)

limits: This is an ATTRIBUTION + SOURCE-POINTER layer, not a raw address dump. Government attributions (FBI/OFAC/Treasury/DOJ) are high-grade; chain-forensics clustering is labeled probabilistic. Literal hex addresses are NOT hand-transcribed (error risk) - the canonical machine-readable source is the OFAC SDN 'Digital Currency Address' set plus per-incident FBI PSA IOC lists, with a wired fetch_ofac.py roadmap to ingest them for live graph cross-referencing. Sanction status is time-varying and dated (Tornado Cash 2022->vacated 2024->lifted 2025). No private individuals are accused beyond those already charged/convicted in the public record.
